title: The Null Problem
description: Understand the billion-dollar mistake that crashes apps.

slides.title: What is Null? 🕳️
slides.content:
## The Empty Box

Imagine a variable as a box. Usually, you put a Number or a String inside it. 

But sometimes, the box contains **absolutely nothing**. It is completely empty. In programming, this state of 'nothingness' is called `null`.

slides.title: The Billion Dollar Mistake 💥
slides.content:
In early languages like Java or C, if you tried to ask an empty `null` box to perform an action (e.g., "Hey empty box, print your text!"), the program would instantly **CRASH**.

This is known as a **NullPointerException (NPE)**. 
The creator of `null` famously called it his "Billion Dollar Mistake" because it has caused countless apps to crash globally over the decades.

quizData.question: What happens in older legacy languages if you try to perform a direct action/method on a variable that currently contains `null`?
quizData.explanation: Attempting to access properties or functions on an object that literally doesn't exist triggers the infamous fatal NullPointerException system crash.

slides.title: Kotlin's Promise 🛡️
slides.content:
## Built-in Armor

Kotlin's primary selling point is that it aims to **eliminate NullPointerExceptions entirely**.

It does this by making nullability an explicit, loudly declared part of the language structure.
